title: Use breadcrumbs on pages three or more levels deep
summary: AI-generated multi-page apps often lack breadcrumb navigation, disorienting users in deep hierarchies.
instruction: Any page three or more levels deep in the site hierarchy should display breadcrumb navigation showing the path from the top level.
rationale: Breadcrumbs reduce disorientation and support backtracking. They provide context for deep-linked pages where users arrive without surrounding navigation.

violations: A product detail page 3 levels deep with no breadcrumb.
A documentation page 5 levels deep with no breadcrumb.
controls: An e-commerce page with 'Home > Electronics > Headphones > Sony WH-1000XM5'.
A docs page with 'Docs > API > Authentication > OAuth2'.
borderline: Single-page applications where 'level' is ambiguous should still provide context.

exceptions.description: Dashboards where the user's mental model is flat. Marketing landing pages that are single-level. Mobile-first apps with bottom tab navigation.

detector.signalDescription: Check URL path depth. If 3+ segments, check for nav with aria-label='breadcrumb' or breadcrumb-structured data.
limitations: SPA hash routing makes URL depth unreliable.
Alternative navigation context (tabs, steps) may serve the same purpose.

scenarios: Flat sites where URL depth does not match navigation depth.
Wizard/multi-step forms where step indicators replace breadcrumbs.
mitigations: Check for step indicators and tab navigation as alternative context.

fixturePlan.description: An e-commerce product page at /products/electronics/headphones/sony with no breadcrumb.
Same page with breadcrumb showing the full path.
A settings page at /settings/account/security with tab-based section navigation.
